keyPoints: Frictionful workflows boost task quality and originality for novices, with higher cognitive load.
Novices rely on scaffolding to develop planning, critical thinking, and domain voice.
Experts gain speed with frictionless drafting but still need friction at critical decisions to preserve originality.
Adaptive friction design: tailor friction level to task phase and user expertise.
Provenance, justification, and explicit evaluation prompts strengthen trust and accountability.
Use multi-method metrics (originality, task quality, cognitive load) and report subgroup effects.
Develop domain-specific use protocols and a friction-design library for practitioners.
